title: Classes, Modifiers, Private State, and Abstract Contracts · Mastery Check
summary: Implement scheduling components with runtime invariants, replaceable policies, and explicit construction rules. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a typed scheduling component library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish instance-side and static-side class types, fields, methods, and constructor signatures.
Use public, protected, TypeScript private, and JavaScript hash-private fields with their actual compile-time and runtime boundaries.
Use parameter properties only when concise construction does not hide validation or ownership transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.

steps.title: Class Instance Static · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A scheduling record has id "job-6", an optional owner, readonly creation time, and a mutable status transition. Reject a misspelled status field, protect the invariant, and show which readonly promise exists only at compile time. Competency probe: Distinguish instance-side and static-side class types, fields, methods, and constructor signatures.
